items.headline: Equinix data center receives $138M permit in San José
items.summary: A major data center expansion at 131 Great Oaks Boulevard is cleared to build, representing one of the largest single construction projects permitted in the city. Data centers drive local tax revenue and job creation but also consume significant electricity and water resources.

items.headline: Phase B construction permitted for PLD Athena logistics hub
items.summary: Site work begins on the second phase of what appears to be a large logistics or industrial complex at 5853 Rue Ferrari, with a $35M permit issued.

items.headline: City Council to vote on Seeds of Peace Monument at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: The City Council will consider an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust to install Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by Mario Chiodo, at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The agreement governs the terms of placement and maintenance of this public art installation.

items.headline: Actions on Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District budget
items.summary: The City Council will act on the FY 2026-27 budget report and annual assessment for the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District, which funds improvements and services in that commercial corridor.

items.headline: General Plan health and safety element update scheduled
items.summary: The City Council is considering an update to the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan (2015-2040). The agenda does not provide details on what specific changes are proposed or how they would affect residents.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent city manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent city manager. This ends the interim or acting period for the top municipal executive position.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates Urban Water Master Plan and shortage contingency rules
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed the draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. These documents set the city's water management strategy and rules for rationing during droughts.

items.headline: City opens spring funding cycle for community events and neighborhood grants
items.summary: The City Council considered applications for the 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program spring cycle on May 18. Residents and organizations can apply for funding to support local events and neighborhood projects.
